lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Live Test Protocol ]─ ┐

Live Test Protocol

Goal

Run controlled live testing of multi-tier Lore usage in two stages:

  1. Pilot (Roadmap Phase 1): 3-5 days, 1-2 people, 2-tier only — catch UX friction early
  2. Full test (Roadmap Phase 3): 1 week, full team, 2-tier and optionally 3-tier — validate at scale

Both stages validate:

  • Zero data loss during sync (absolute requirement)
  • Conflicts are detected and recoverable
  • PR-first contributions work end-to-end
  • Knowledge propagation improves session acceleration across members

Preflight Gate (Required Before Stage 1)

Pilot and live test execution are blocked until baseline truth gates pass on main. These checks also run in CI (.github/workflows/validate.yml) on every push and PR.

Current status: baseline preflight gates are green on main after the pilot-readiness hardening wave.

GateCommandWhat it catches
Doc claimsbash scripts/validate-doc-claims.shDocs/code behavior drift
Consistencybash scripts/validate-consistency.shRegistry/nav/link/frontmatter drift
Smoke testsbash tests/test-scripts-smoke.shScript regressions, generated-repo parity
Hook testsbash tests/test-hooks-unit.shHook behavior regressions
E2E testsbash tests/test-multi-tier-e2e.shMulti-tier deploy/sync/contribute chain
MkDocs buildmkdocs buildDocumentation build failures

If any preflight check fails, fix-forward is required before pilot starts.

Stage 1: Pilot Test (Roadmap Phase 1)

Duration: 3-5 days Participants: 1-2 people (operator + one team member) Topology: 2-tier only (team root → instance)

Pilot Setup

  1. Deploy a fresh team-root repo using deploy-instance.sh with level: team
  2. Deploy 1-2 instance repos from the team root
  3. Verify validate-consistency.sh passes at each level
  4. Document the exact deploy commands and time taken
  5. Verify generated repos include expected docs payload (including docs/examples/)

Pilot Instrumentation (Required)

  1. Use scripts/pilot-log-event.py to log key events in real time
  2. Record at minimum each day:
    • deploy_minutes (day 1 only)
    • daily_routine_minutes
    • sync_conflict (if any)
    • conflict_resolution_minutes (if any)
    • script_failure (if any)
    • skill_created
    • skill_contributed
    • propagation_hours (when measured)
  3. Generate daily summary with scripts/pilot-generate-report.py
  4. Attach generated data/report.md to pilot check-in notes

Pilot Focus Areas

  • First-run experience: Can someone deploy an instance from written docs alone?
  • Daily workflow friction: Where do people get stuck during sync/reconcile/contribute?
  • Error message quality: Are failures actionable without reading source code?
  • Sync safety: Does sync preserve local changes correctly?
  • Direct mode behavior (experimental): When --direct is used in high-trust tests, is behavior predictable, reversible, and auditable?

Direct Mode Experimental Track (Pilot)

Run a controlled subset of operations using --direct with LORE_ALLOW_DIRECT_PUSH=1.

Requirements:

  1. Use only on explicitly designated pilot branches/environments
  2. Record each use in pilot logs with:
    • reason direct mode was chosen
    • operation performed
    • outcome and any recovery steps
  3. Immediately switch back to PR mode for any non-trivial conflict/failure

Pilot Exit Criteria

  • Deploy team + instance from scratch without manual intervention
  • Complete 3 full sync cycles without data loss
  • Complete 1 PR-based contribution (instance → team → merge → sync back)
  • Document all friction points and blockers
  • No critical or high-severity issues found (or all are fixed before Roadmap Phase 2)
  • Preflight truth gates remain green throughout pilot window
  • Direct mode trial outcomes are documented and do not produce unrecoverable incidents

Pilot Findings Template

For each friction point discovered:

**What:** [brief description]
**When:** [during which operation — deploy, sync, contribute, reconcile]
**Severity:** [blocker / friction / cosmetic]
**Fix:** [immediate fix or deferred to which phase]

Stage 2: Full Live Test (Roadmap Phase 3)

Duration: 1 week Participants: Full team Topology: 2-tier (required) + 3-tier (optional, if pilot succeeded)

Test Topologies

Topology A: 2-tier (required)

Team root → Instances (2-3 members)

Topology B: 3-tier (optional)

Org → Team → Instances (only attempt if 2-tier pilot was clean)

Roles

  • Maintainer (Team/Org): reviews and merges PRs, resolves conflicts when needed
  • Member (Instance): daily usage, contributes useful skills/docs upward
  • Observer (optional): captures friction points and failure modes without participating

Daily Schedule (Recommended)

Each member (instance):

  1. Start of day

    • Run /sync-from-team
    • If 3-tier: team runs /sync-from-org weekly (or mid-week)

  2. During work

    • Work normally on external projects
    • When a gotcha happens: create a skill
    • When a multi-step procedure emerges: write a runbook

  3. End of day

    • Run /reconcile
    • Contribute selected items upward (/contribute-to-team)

Maintainer:

  • Review contributions daily (aim for < 24h turnaround)
  • Merge safe, validated changes
  • Post a short changelog to the team (what changed, what to sync)

Daily Health Check

Run at each level, every day:

scripts/validate-consistency.sh

Log the output. Any failures must be investigated same-day.

Metrics to Capture

Safety / Reliability (Quantitative)

MetricTargetHow to measure
Silent overwritesZeroCheck .lore-sync-conflicts/ after each sync
Sync conflicts detectedTrack countCount conflict snapshots per sync
Conflict resolution time< 15 minTime from detection to resolved
Ledger rebuilds neededTrack countNote each rebuild and its cause
Script failuresTrack countAny non-zero exit from sync/contribute

Workflow / UX (Quantitative)

MetricTargetHow to measure
Fresh instance deploy< 10 minTime from git clone to passing validation
Daily sync + reconcile< 5 minTime for full daily routine
Friction pointsDocument allStructured notes per pilot findings template

Knowledge Propagation (Quantitative)

MetricTargetHow to measure
Skills created>= 3 per memberCount skill files created during test
Skills contributed>= 3 totalCount PRs or direct pushes with skills
Discovery → availability< 48hTime from skill creation to team merge

Session Acceleration (Qualitative)

  • Did the agent re-ask previously answered questions? (Should not, if skills exist)
  • Did new members benefit from inherited skills/docs?
  • Rate 1-5: “How much did inherited knowledge help your first session?”

Failure Handling

Sync conflicts

  1. Do not delete conflict snapshots
  2. Run bash scripts/list-sync-conflicts.sh to see all conflicts
  3. Review each conflict and choose local or parent version
  4. Run bash scripts/resolve-sync-conflicts.sh if applicable
  5. Document the conflict cause in daily log

Ledger missing or corrupted

  1. Run bash scripts/rebuild-sync-ledger.sh --verify first (check without modifying)
  2. If verify shows issues, run bash scripts/rebuild-sync-ledger.sh to rebuild
  3. Document the incident: what caused it, how it was recovered

Unrecoverable state

If an instance is in an unrecoverable state:

  1. Do not delete the instance — preserve for post-mortem
  2. Deploy a fresh instance from the team root
  3. Manually copy any locally-created skills/docs from the broken instance
  4. Document the incident in detail (this is a critical finding)

Rollback Strategy

Pilot rollback

If pilot reveals fundamental issues:

  1. Stop the pilot
  2. Document all findings
  3. Create new plan items for fixes
  4. Restart pilot after fixes land

Full test rollback

If full test reveals issues mid-week:

  1. All members stop syncing (freeze state)
  2. Maintainer evaluates: fix-forward or abort
  3. If abort: preserve all instances for post-mortem, fall back to standalone usage
  4. Document findings as decision record in this plan’s folder

Success Criteria

Must-have (test fails without these)

  • Zero silent overwrites of locally modified files during sync
  • Conflicts always captured into .lore-sync-conflicts/ and resolvable
  • PR-first contributions create reviewable PRs that merge cleanly
  • All contribute script failures reported (no silent failures)
  • One full week of real usage with no data loss

Should-have (strong signal of readiness)

  • At least 3 skills contributed and merged across tiers during test
  • At least 1 meaningful example of cross-member session acceleration
  • Fresh instance deploys in < 10 minutes from written docs
  • Daily sync + reconcile routine takes < 5 minutes
  • No friction points rated as “blocker” remain unfixed

Related Plans

  • Generated Repo Parity Hardening (archived in lorehq-legacy)
  • CI Truth Gates (archived in lorehq-legacy)
